Title: How China uses influencers to polish its image
Subhead: Chongqing, a futuristic Chinese city, has caught the eye of influencers and bloggers. Beijing, meanwhile, welcomes new visitors, often unaware of the geopolitical and ideological currents running beneath their social media posts.

Heatwave in France: 'This is an unprecedented event, with about a one-in-1,000 chance of occurring in a given year'
Subhead: The research director explains the climatic conditions that led to France's record-breaking temperatures during this season, and emphasizes the urgent need for political action against global warming.

Title: 'With his encyclical, Leo XIV does not simply proclaim that another world is possible: He calls on us to build that other world'
Subhead: Following the pope's release of the first encyclical of his pontificate, 'Magnifica humanitas,' on Monday, historian of Catholicism Alberto Melloni analyzes the significance of this original document, which focuses on artificial intelligence.

Title: Gaël Monfils bids farewell to the French Open, closing a chapter in French tennis history
Subhead: In his final appearance at the Roland-Garros stadium, the Frenchman, who plans to retire at the end of the season, was eliminated in the first round on Monday by his compatriot Hugo Gaston. Having delivered some of his most memorable matches on the Parisian clay, he leaves behind the legacy of a truly exceptional showman.

Title: At the Grand Palais, Hilma af Klint, a pioneer of abstraction
Subhead: Before Wassily Kandinsky or Piet Mondrian, the Swedish artist created breathtaking geometric and symbolic works infused with esoteric references.

A €100,000 contract and possible mistaken identity: New clues emerge in murder of anti-drug activist's brother
Subhead: The case file shows that the Marseille murder stemmed from major organized crime, involving several competing teams of hitmen. According to some suspects' statements, it was Amine Kessaci and not his brother Mehdi who was targeted, due to his commitment to fighting drug trafficking.

Title: Kyiv attacks: The complex logistics network behind Russia's war machine
Subhead: The Ukrainian capital endured a barrage of strikes over the weekend, including ballistic missiles, cruise missiles and long-range drones. These increasingly frequent hybrid operations reveal the intensification of Russian drone and missile production.

Title: Why dropping the worst-case climate scenario does not undermine the reality of global warming
Subhead: A new generation of climate scenarios has set aside RCP8.5, which projected a nearly 5°C rise in global temperatures by 2100. Donald Trump presents this as proof of 'alarmism' among climate scientists, but the new projections remain highly concerning.
